user_message: Just got this from Steven Wilson at Tailspin Toys -- I need help figuring out pipeline hygiene. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-08 16:57 EST
Subject: Follow-up: pipeline hygiene Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-04 11:07 EST
Subject: URGENT: pipeline hygiene Issue at Tailspin Toys

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with pipeline hygiene. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-06 15:46 EST
Subject: Re: Re: pipeline hygiene Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for pipeline hygiene. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Tailspin Toys: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-L trap (Stakeholder-map refresh as separate proposal vs inline verification); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-L failure mode (Stakeholder-map refresh as separate proposal vs inline verification)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-L -- Stakeholder-map refresh as separate proposal vs inline verification
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
